message: bd-3gpn: honor force_terminal for color detection

 {"id":"bd-2rqt","title":"API misuse detection tests","description":"## Problem
Users can easily misuse the API (e.g., using Cell::new() instead of Cell::from_markup()). We need tests that document these gotchas and ensure the API behaves predictably.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document all methods that accept strings but DON'T parse markup
- [ ] Document all methods that DO parse markup  
- [ ] Add tests that explicitly show the difference
- [ ] Consider adding deprecation warnings or lints for confusing APIs

## API Audit checklist
- [ ] Cell::new() - does NOT parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Cell::from_markup() - DOES parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Text::new() - does NOT parse markup
- [ ] Text::from_markup() - DOES parse markup
- [ ] Panel title/content - audit and document
- [ ] Tree labels - audit and document
- [ ] Progress descriptions - audit and document

## Files to modify
- Add doc comments to all relevant structs/methods
- tests/conformance/*.rs - add explicit documentation tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:09.661046714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238099951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238082187Z","close_reason":"Documented markup parsing behavior across Text/Panel/Tree/Progress/Table; added conformance tests for Text vs markup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2sa2","title":"Style verification test helpers","description":"## Problem
Current tests often just check that output is non-empty or doesn't crash. We need test helpers that can actually VERIFY styles were applied correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create test_utils module with helper functions for style verification
- [ ] Helper to check if output contains ANSI color codes
- [ ] Helper to check if specific style was applied (bold, italic, color)
- [ ] Helper to extract styled segments from rendered output
- [ ] Document how to use these helpers in tests

## Implementation Notes
- Could check for ANSI escape sequences like \x1b[1m for bold
- Could provide segment inspection utilities
- Make it easy to write assertions like assert_has_bold!(output)

## Files to create/modify
- tests/test_utils.rs or tests/common/mod.rs - create helpers
- Update existing tests to use these helpers where appropriate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:29.751175627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109472650Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109159270Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive style verification helpers to tests/common/assertions.rs: ansi module with constants, style assertions (bold/italic/underline/color), strip_ansi, and extract_styled_regions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2thu","title":"E2E tests: markup parsing across all renderables","description":"## Problem
The table cell markup parsing bug (where [bold #2ecc71]PLAY[/] showed literally instead of being styled) was not caught by existing tests. This indicates a gap in our testing strategy - we need E2E tests that verify markup is actually parsed and rendered correctly across ALL renderables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create E2E tests that verify markup tags are NOT present in final rendered output
- [ ] Test markup parsing in: Table cells, Panel titles/content, Text, Tree nodes, Progress bar descriptions
- [ ] Each test should render to a string buffer and assert markup tags like [bold], [red], etc. are NOT in output
- [ ] Tests should verify styles ARE applied (check for ANSI codes or styled segments)

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Pattern: render content with markup -> assert raw markup tags absent -> assert styling present
- These are integration tests, not unit tests

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests  
- tests/conformance/text_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- May need to create new test modules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:26.168768880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690653345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690621495Z","close_reason":"Added E2E markup parsing tests across renderables; validated e2e_rendering","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
